summary: Hardened M6 Alpha core-flow accessibility within client/web/test surfaces: M6 command and checkpoint statuses are polite live regions, policy/event choices and terminal/adviser/reason surfaces expose text/list semantics, the map read-model viewport is keyboard focusable with arrow/Home/End district navigation, focus-visible and reduced-motion CSS are explicit, and Playwright/Vitest now cover keyboard, focus order, non-color status, screen-reader label spot checks, reduced-motion, and viewport overflow. React still consumes read-model slices and command DTOs only; no full WorldState, sim-core rule change, telemetry, account, network, Electron IPC, or manual node battle UI was introduced.

tests_run.result: Initial status on feat/m6-accessibility-core-flows showed only route/state metadata changes from lead/orchestration; no unexpected package-code changes were present before implementation.
Initial run exposed that the M6 error-state primary goal was not rendered; fixed by displaying snapshot.goal.primaryGoal in the M6 scenario panel.
1 test file passed; 8 tests passed, including M6 Alpha live-region, policy choice, empty/error/extreme, list semantics, and no-WorldState SSR coverage.
Vite web build passed. Existing chunk-size warning was reported; no new dependency or bundle-split decision was introduced.
Storybook build completed successfully. Existing chunk-size warning was reported.
First run showed the new reduced-motion assertion depended on Chromium CSS duration string units; fixed by parsing ms/s values numerically.

Second run showed initial browser/document focus did not consistently enter the page on the first Tab in this environment; adjusted the smoke to set the first map control as the focus-order starting point, then verify Tab/Enter/Arrow keyboard behavior.

11 Chromium Playwright tests passed. New M6 accessibility smoke covers map control focus order, map viewport keyboard navigation, M6 scenario start, command preview/confirm, save/load checkpoint, live status, non-color data-status-kind states, reduced-motion, policy option text, and existing required viewport overflow checks at 1280x720, 1280x800, 1920x1080, and 2560x1080.
Architecture boundaries, sim determinism, full root Vitest suite, and web package tests passed: 40 root test files / 305 tests plus web 1 file / 4 tests.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive package typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed on the final diff.

decisions: Kept the work in existing React DOM, CSS, client-core read-model fixtures, and Playwright/Vitest patterns; no new production dependency was added.
Made M6 command and save statuses persistent polite live regions with explicit data-status-kind values so state is not conveyed only by color.

Exposed M6 policy/event choices, resolved event state, encyclopedia content tags, terminal conditions, adviser candidates, and reason codes through textual/list semantics while still only consuming read-model values and reason codes.

Added keyboard navigation to the map renderer host as a focusable read-model region that cycles district selection through existing client selection callbacks; it does not mutate authoritative WorldState or make React own Pixi DisplayObjects.

Did not add .storybook stories because .storybook/** is outside the allowed path list for this route. Existing Storybook build passes; normal/empty/error/extreme M6 state coverage is provided by tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx and core-flow behavior by tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts.
Preserved DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE: the UI continues to state manual node battle is absent and no manual battle implementation or spike surface was added.
risks: This is Alpha core-flow accessibility hardening only, not final M7/M8 accessibility completion, localization coverage, or platform accessibility certification.
Manual assistive-technology review was not performed in this implementation thread; evidence is automated role/name/live-region/focus/status spot checks plus recorded limitations.
The Playwright focus-order smoke starts from the first map mode control because Chromium in this environment did not reliably focus the page from the first Tab after navigation.

Storybook state matrix remains limited by the allowed path list for this route; QA should treat the passing Storybook build and Vitest/E2E state coverage as the evidence for this task, not as final complex-UI Storybook completeness.
requested_action: Independently review M6-ACCESSIBILITY-CORE-FLOWS-001 for Alpha core-flow accessibility evidence, keyboard/focus/live-region/non-color/viewport coverage, read-model and command/query boundaries, forbidden path compliance, and the recorded limits. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK; do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED.
